message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Workshop Kitchen + Bar (Palm Springs)
Cuisine/type signal: New American / farm-to-table
Why it surfaced in discovery: Michelin Guide-recommended; first Palm Springs restaurant to win a James Beard Foundation Outstanding Restaurant Design award (2015, SOMA Architects). Consistently anchors regional best-of fine-dining lists.
Discovery sources: https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/workshop-kitchen-bar-becomes-palm-springs-1st-james-beard-award-winner-for-design/ | https://guide.michelin.com/us/en/california/palm-springs/restaurant/workshop-kitchen-bar | https://www.workshopkitchenbar.com/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

# Workshop Kitchen & Bar - Business Information

**Business Name:** Workshop Kitchen & Bar

**Address:** 800 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92262, United States

**Phone:** 760.459.3451

**Email:** palmsprings@workshopkitchenbar.com

**Hours of Operation:**
- Sunday Brunch: Daily 10am–2pm
- Dinner: Daily 5pm–10pm

---

## Menu Structure
The restaurant offers five menu categories:
1. **Dinner** – "Smalls" (appetizers, $22–$28) and "Plates" (entrees, $32–$52)
2. **Brunch** – Small plates ($12–$20) and Plates ($18–$34)
3. **Bar** – Original cocktails ($18), beer ($7–$9)
4. **Wine List** – Extensive selection by glass and bottle, plus corkage fees ($35–$50)
5. **Market Soup & Wood-Fired Pizza** – Nightly selections (market price)

---

## Awards & Credentials
- **Michelin Recommended** restaurant
- **James Beard Foundation Award** – "Outstanding Restaurant Design" (2015)
- **Restaurant & Bar Design Awards** – "Best Design for the Americas" (2013)

---

## Reservation & Booking
- **Reservation System:** Resy (direct link available on homepage)
- **Events/Catering:** Dedicated website (workshopkitchencatering.com); contact eventsps@workshopkitchenbar.com

---

## Social Media Links
- Instagram: @workshopkitchenbar
- Facebook and X (Twitter) accounts linked in footer

---

## Additional Information
- **Chef/Owner:** Michael Beckman
- **Co-Owner:** Joseph Mourani
- **Cuisine Style:** Elevated California cuisine with farm-to-table focus, wood-fired cooking
- **Notable Features:** Historic 1926 El Paseo building; modernist concrete interior design; chef's gardens in Rancho Mirage and on-site

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Workshop Kitchen & Bar in Palm Springs and its Michelin Guide recognition for 2025:

## Michelin Guide Recognition

Workshop Kitchen & Bar is a restaurant in the 2025 MICHELIN Guide USA. More specifically, the restaurant has received consecutive Michelin Plate recognition in 2024 and 2025.

## Restaurant Overview

Located at 800 N. Palm Canyon Dr., the restaurant features Californian cuisine and showcases farm-to-table food that draws on the bounty of the nearby Coachella Valley, one of the nation's most productive agricultural regions.

A dedicated garden in Rancho Mirage, along with a sizeable herb planter behind the kitchen, furnishes peak-season produce for wood-fired dishes like a flat iron au poivre, and even a simple yuzu tart is prettied up with farm-fresh raspberries and wood sorrel.

## Ambiance & Design

The setting is a Spanish-style building from the 1920s, while the interior is a modernist temple of polished concrete—a mist-cooled courtyard provides additional seating.

## Additional Accolades

The restaurant is a 4-time Michelin recommended and James Beard awarded restaurant in Palm Springs.

Based on the search results, here's an overview of Workshop Kitchen & Bar in Palm Springs according to Yelp and other reviews:

## Restaurant Overview

Workshop Kitchen & Bar was established in 2012, and chef/owner Michael Beckman and Joseph Mourani partnered for a unique combination of seasonal cooking, cocktails and design. It's a 4-time Michelin recommended & James Beard awarded restaurant in Palm Springs.

## Location & Ambiance

The restaurant is located at 800 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92262. It has a central location, unique ambience and service and is certainly creative. It's a large space broken up into intimate small ones with various seating arrangements including a long communal table, with nicely designed booths and concrete and wood modern interior design.

## Yelp Reviews - Mixed Opinions

The reviews appear to be mixed:

**Positive reviews:** Some diners reported wonderful evenings with great service and food, enjoying the patio on lovely evenings. One reviewer noted that brunch became more than a meal, with design, conversation, and inventive cooking coming together.

**Negative reviews:** Some diners found the experience disappointing and don't plan to return on future visits. One reviewer called their special dinner "horrible on every level", citing issues with food quality and service.

The restaurant clearly has passionate supporters as well as critics, with experiences varying considerably.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Workshop Kitchen + Bar in Palm Springs and its 2015 James Beard Award:

Workshop Kitchen + Bar, located in the historic El Paseo Building on North Palm Canyon, became Palm Springs' first James Beard award winner for Outstanding Restaurant Design in the category of over 75 seats. The prestigious award was given to the architecture firm SOMA Architects for best Restaurant Design or Renovation in North America.

Designer Michel Abboud of SOMA Architects created the interior design. The design approach was a simple insertion of poured in place cast concrete brutalist elements using an ecclesiastic design approach.

The restaurant was designed for chef owners Michael Beckman and Joe Mourani and was completed in October of 2012. The award was presented at the 2015 James Beard Awards Gala & Reception on May 4 at the Lyric Opera of Chicago.

The menu focuses on American bistro classics with global influences. The restaurant has earned the TripAdvisor Traveler's Choice Award and Wine Spectator Award of Excellence in 2024.

**Eight4Nine Restaurant & Lounge**
Eight4Nine Restaurant & Lounge is a trendy dining spot located in the Uptown Design District of Palm Springs. Its menu features refined New American cuisine with Pacific Coast influences, offering dishes like ahi tuna poke, steelhead nicoise salad, and mesquite-smoked carne asada.

**Jake's Palm Springs**
Jake's, the hip, casual American bistro in the Uptown Design District of Palm Springs. You can dine al fresco in their charming courtyard or intimate dining room, with the front patio facing Palm Canyon Drive and getting the best view of the mountains.

All three of these restaurants are well-regarded New American establishments in the Uptown Design District, each with its own unique atmosphere and culinary approach.

input.prompt: Total review count, average rating, recent sentiment, any awards/badges (Diners' Choice, etc.), cuisine description.
Total review count, rating, ranking among Palm Springs restaurants, recent reviewer sentiment.

content.content:
# Workshop Kitchen & Bar - Palm Springs Summary

**Overview:** Workshop is a modern Californian restaurant helmed by chef Michael Beckman, located at 800 N Palm Canyon Dr in Palm Springs. The establishment features "architectural concrete features in a soaring space from 1927" and holds a MICHELIN Guide rating for good cooking.

**Rating & Ranking:** 4.0 out of 5 stars based on 831 reviews, ranked #41 of 281 restaurants in Palm Springs.

**Cuisine & Features:** Specializing in seafood and contemporary cuisine, the restaurant emphasizes wood-fired dishes and farm-to-table ingredients from the Coachella Valley. The space includes both indoor dining with modernist design and outdoor patio seating.

**Service & Experience:** Reviews are mixed. While some diners praise exceptional service and describe it as "one of the best restaurant[s] in Southern California," others report rushed service and difficulty with wait times. One reviewer noted nearly two-hour waits for entrees.

**Pricing:** Listed in the $$$$ (high-end) category.

**Hours:** Open for dinner only; closed Wednesdays. Sunday hours include lunch service (10 AM-2 PM) and dinner (5-10 PM).

**Highlights:** The restaurant earned a James Beard Award for best restaurant design in 2015 and maintains consecutive Michelin stars.

content.content:
# Schema.org and Metadata Analysis

Based on my review of the provided HTML content, **I found no JSON-LD structured data** using schema.org markup for Restaurant, LocalBusiness, or related types.

## Missing Implementations

The page lacks:
- Restaurant schema with address, phone, hours
- Menu schema with dish details
- Organization/LocalBusiness structured data
- Opening hours specifications (though hours appear in plain text)

## Detected Meta Elements

The content provided shows:
- Basic page title: "Workshop Kitchen | Michelin Recommended Restaurant Palm Springs"
- No explicit Open Graph tags visible in the extracted content
- No microdata (itemscope/itemprop) attributes detected

## Contact & Hours in Plain Text Only

Rather than machine-readable markup, the site presents critical information as human-readable text:

> "800 N Palm Canyon Dr Palm Springs CA 92262, United States"

> "Sunday Brunch Everyday - 10am-2pm, 5pm-10pm"

**Recommendation**: Adding JSON-LD schema markup would improve SEO visibility, enable rich snippets in search results, and provide better accessibility for search engines and screen readers.

Based on the search results, here are some great date night restaurant options in the Uptown area of Palm Springs:

## Uptown Design District Restaurants

Trio Restaurant, located in the vibrant Uptown Design District of Palm Springs, is a local favorite known for its solid American cuisine and specialty cocktails. The restaurant features warm interior design with orange, browns, and beige palette with luminous sea shell, spherical lighting, and its signature rubber-wrapped chandelier.

Birba is located in the Uptown Design District and features a beautiful tree-filled patio with fire pits and a wood-fired oven producing exceptional pizzas. Since 2011, they've specialized in homemade Milanese and Tuscan-style food, sourcing ingredients from small, local farms, including a signature appetizer of squash blossoms stuffed with ricotta.

1501 Uptown Gastropub is a great option for brunch, where visitors are drawn by the crab Benedict.

The Uptown Design District features trendy spots like Birba and Trio. Both restaurants offer excellent atmospheres and menus suitable for a romantic date night experience.

input.name: Workshop Kitchen + Bar
input.corridor: Uptown Design District (N. Palm Canyon Dr.)
input.cuisine_or_type: New American / Modern Californian, farm-to-table, wood-fired

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 1,596 reviews; Google 1,250+ reviews; OpenTable 1,788 ratings; Tripadvisor 831 reviews — aggregate 5,000+ across platforms
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive with notable mixed signal (Yelp/Tripadvisor reviews split between exceptional and disappointed; Google 4.3 and OpenTable 4.4 anchor positive)
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews continue to praise the design, wood-fired plates, cocktails, and brunch; recurring critiques focus on service pacing (rushed or slow), perceived value at the price point, and occasional inconsistency. No signal of closure or major decline — restaurant active through 2026.
reputation.longevity: Operating since October 2012 (13+ years); original chef/owner Michael Beckman and co-owner Joseph Mourani still attached per current site — ownership stable
credentials: James Beard Foundation Award — Outstanding Restaurant Design, 75+ seats (2015, awarded to SOMA Architects for the space)
Michelin Guide recommended — multi-year (described as 4-time Michelin recommended; Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025)
Restaurant & Bar Design Awards — Best Design for the Americas (2013)
Featured by Palm Springs Life, Hotels Above Par, Time Out Palm Springs and regional best-of fine-dining lists

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably for 'Michelin restaurants Palm Springs', 'James Beard Palm Springs', 'fine dining Palm Springs', and 'best restaurants Palm Springs uptown design district'. Less reliably surfaced in broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' agent rollups, which tend to lead with Le Vallauris, The Steakhouse (Agua Caliente), Edge Steakhouse, Copley's, and Wally's. Does not surface for cuisine-specific queries like 'New American near Palm Springs' as consistently as its reputation would suggest — agents often default to Eight4Nine, Trio, and Jake's for that bracket.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Generally accurate when it does surface: Michelin-recommended, James Beard design winner, farm-to-table California cuisine, uptown design district address. Some agent summaries conflate 'Michelin Plate / Recommended' with 'Michelin star' or 'consecutive Michelin stars' (Tripadvisor summary above made this error) — a real hallucination risk to watch.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Weak. workshopkitchenbar.com presents address, hours, and menu in human-readable text only. No schema.org JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup detected, no clear Open Graph or microdata. Hours wording ('Sunday Brunch Everyday 10am-2pm, 5pm-10pm') is ambiguous for parsers. Resy handles reservations off-site. Strong human design, weak agent-readable signal.

competitors: Le Vallauris (Palm Springs) — classic fine dining anchor
Copley's on Palm Canyon (Palm Springs) — historic-home fine dining peer
Eight4Nine (Palm Springs uptown) — refined New American, direct corridor competitor
Trio Palm Springs (uptown design district) — American bistro, same-corridor competitor at a lower price tier
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — Workshop is the design-and-credential anchor of the Uptown Design District restaurant cluster; Trio, Eight4Nine, Birba and Jake's read as supporting venues in the same corridor
query_wins: 'uptown design district fine dining Palm Springs'

convertibility.ownership_signal: independent — chef/owner Michael Beckman and co-owner Joseph Mourani, single-location Palm Springs operation (an LA outpost on La Brea is closed per Yelp)
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Active branded site (workshopkitchenbar.com) plus a separate catering site (workshopkitchencatering.com) and a Palm Springs sub-route — suggests in-house or contracted web work. Active Instagram (@workshopkitchenbar), Facebook, X. Paid presence on Resy, OpenTable, Yelp, Tripadvisor. Earned-media footprint in Palm Springs Life, Hotels Above Par, Descanso Resort blog, and regional guides — consistent with a venue that invests in PR. No agency footprint detected, but design-forward brand discipline is evident.
convertibility.reasoning: Workshop is an owner-operator independent with credentialed reputation, an active multi-site web presence, paid platform listings, and demonstrable PR activity. Chef/owner Michael Beckman and Joseph Mourani are reachable via the site's posted email and phone. The venue clearly invests in brand and marketing yet ships a website with no schema.org markup, ambiguous hours formatting, and weak machine-readable location/menu signal — a textbook convertible: high reputation, real spend, fixable agent-visibility gap.

flags: Michelin status: secondary sources describe Workshop as 'Michelin recommended' / 'Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025'. Direct Michelin Guide page returned 403 and could not be confirmed firsthand — [UNVERIFIED] whether the current 2025/2026 designation is Plate vs. Recommended vs. Bib Gourmand. One source mischaracterized it as 'Michelin stars'; that is almost certainly an error and should not be repeated.

Review counts: Yelp 1,596 and Tripadvisor 831 are from search-result snippets, not direct page reads (both 403/timeout). Google '1,250+ reviews / 4.3' and OpenTable '1,788 / 4.4' likewise from snippets — [UNVERIFIED] at the exact number; bands are reliable.

Ownership continuity: site lists Beckman and Mourani as chef/owner and co-owner currently, consistent with 2012 founding — [UNVERIFIED] whether any silent investor or management changes have occurred.

Schema.org check based on WebFetch's converted-markdown view of the homepage. A deeper crawl of inner pages (menu, contact) could reveal markup not present on the landing page — [UNVERIFIED] site-wide.
